hypothesis: A hybrid adaptive batching algorithm that dynamically switches between static-64 and dynamic batching strategies based on a real-time estimate of the current graph-size variance within the training epoch will achieve greater training speedups than either algorithm alone, because high-variance phases (where dynamic batching's tighter memory utilization reduces gradient-update time) and low-variance phases (where static-64's lower bookkeeping overhead dominates) can be exploited selectively, and this switching threshold can be learned or tuned as a hyperparameter that is predictable from the kurtosis of the dataset's node/edge distribution measured prior to training.
